bibliographic.citation: Benjamin Mackerell, 'Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich,' from his manuscript History of Norwich (1737), Norfolk Archaeology, vol. 3 (Norwich: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society, 1852), 315-374.
bibliographic.container_title: Norfolk Archaeology: or Miscellaneous Tracts Relating to the Antiquities of the County of Norfolk
bibliographic.pages: 315-374; CME00122 encodes p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334
bibliographic.publisher: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society; printed by Charles Muskett
bibliographic.title: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich

dateStatements.label: Publication
dateStatements.notes: The inspected volume title page controls this entity date; U-M says 1851 and ADS says 1849.

entities.description: The anonymously framed society publication of Mackerell's antiquarian account. The complete article occupies pp. 315-374; CME00122 encodes p. 315 through Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334, not the continuation lower on that final page.
entities.label: Benjamin Mackerell, Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich (1852)
notes: The inspected article has no personal editor or byline. Its heading and society headnote attribute the account to Mackerell's 1737 manuscript in Hudson Gurney's possession.
The complete article continues after the encoded Yelverton text with Mackerell's commentary, further ordinances, office lists, pageantry, property, and dissolution history.

rights.basis: The article was published in 1852 and is in the U.S. public domain because works published before 1931 are outside the current U.S. copyright term in 2026.
rights.component: The 1852 article, society framing, edition, and typography; later scan excluded

access.accessMethod: Pre-book the NRO searchroom and request NNAS S2/1/1-2; production remains subject to condition and staff confirmation
access.entityLabel: Norfolk Record Office, NNAS S2/1/1-2, Mackerell's manuscript History of Norwich
notes: The catalog flags volume-I spine wear and asks that it be used with care.

access.accessMethod: NRO online document-order form using reference NNAS S2/1/1-2, or written enquiry for a whole-manuscript estimate and terms
access.entityLabel: Norfolk Record Office, NNAS S2/1/1-2, Mackerell's manuscript History of Norwich
access.format: Normally JPEG digital images; whole-object feasibility and specification by estimate
notes: The online service price is GBP 19.98 for up to six consecutive or sample images from one document; NRO gives an estimate for the rest and may charge GBP 60 per hour when more cost-effective. A complete two-volume facsimile may therefore be substantially more expensive and remains subject to condition.

For each of the two Mackerell volumes, specify a complete color object capture: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if offered. Ask NRO to preserve a stable image sequence and any foliation or pagination in the delivery.
Request publication permission and delivery terms separately; NRO asks that publication of facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations be requested in writing to the County Archivist.

rights.basis: No item-level image license is publicly attached because no public facsimile was found. NRO says requests to publish facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations must be made in writing to the County Archivist, and its fees page distinguishes duplication from reproduction fees.
rights.component: Any present or future NRO-supplied digital images of NNAS S2/1/1-2; underlying Mackerell text excluded
notes: The policy says no reproduction fee is charged for print runs of 2,000 or fewer, while website or e-book use is listed at GBP 120; written permission and any delivery-specific terms should still be preserved.

The exact volume, folio, or page locus of the St George account is not stated in the public record.

access.entityLabel: Norfolk Record Office, NCR 17b/9, St George's Company guild book

dateStatements.label: Current catalog date
dateStatements.notes: The aggregate volume date is distinct from the historical dates of copied charters and agreements within it.

entities.description: The surviving guild volume whose current catalog contents match the charter/ordinance and 1452 agreement material transcribed by Mackerell; the Book I/A crosswalk is strongly supported but not explicitly stated by the current catalog.

holding.notes: Mackerell's Book I/A designation is recorded as a proposed historical crosswalk, not an explicit current-catalog equivalence.

entities.label: Norfolk Record Office, NCR 17b/9, St George's Company guild book

physicalDescription.extent: 1 volume; Yelverton's Mediation at f. 189 according to Durkee
physicalDescription.format: Calf-leather binding on exposed original oak boards
notes: The calf is extensively torn and missing, and spine cords are exposed, but the NRO catalog says the text block is tight.

The catalog describes a rubricated calendar, inventories, charter-establishment statement and ordinances, member lists, a copy agreement dated 27 March 1452, assembly minutes, officer lists, and a member's oath.
Catalog languages are English and Latin; CME00122 represents English and Middle English documentary wording only.
physicalDescription.support: Mainly paper with some parchment folios

rights.basis: The relevant wording and surviving volume date to the fifteenth and early sixteenth centuries and predate modern copyright by centuries. NRO custody and reproduction conditions are separate.
rights.component: Underlying medieval charter, ordinances, patent wording, and agreement embodied in the guild book; physical ownership, catalog text, and modern images excluded

rights.basis: No item-level image license is publicly attached because no public facsimile was found. NRO says requests to publish facsimiles, transcripts, or extensive quotations must be made in writing to the County Archivist, and its fees page distinguishes duplication from reproduction fees.
rights.component: Any present or future NRO-supplied digital images of NCR 17b/9; underlying medieval wording excluded

entities.description: A current issue/article catalog record that confirms the complete article page range but supplies an issue year conflicting with the inspected volume.
entities.label: ADS Library record for Norfolk Archaeology 03 and the Mackerell article
notes: ADS gives volume 3 (1), article pp. 315-374, and issue year 1849, and links Internet Archive item norfolkarchaeol07socigoog. The direct volume-3 title page in the cached source says 1852.

next_steps: Request a volume/folio crosswalk for the Mackerell account from NRO.
Ask NRO to confirm the Book I/A-to-NCR 17b/9 equivalence and identify each represented document's exact locus.
Keep Durkee's NCR 17b/9 f. 189 identification scoped only to Yelverton's Mediation until other loci are independently verified.
openQuestions.question: Where exactly in NNAS S2/1/1-2 is Mackerell's St George account, and can NRO confirm that Book I marked A is current NCR 17b/9 and supply exact loci for the first ordinances and 1417 patent?

next_steps: Ask NRO whether complete existing digital surrogates already exist before commissioning new photography.
Where condition and cost permit, request the complete two-volume Mackerell manuscript and complete NCR 17b/9 rather than cropped work excerpts.
Preserve the full delivery, inventory, checksum, citation, permission, and pricing terms, then map the work portions inside the unchanged complete objects.
Do not infer image completeness from a small online sample or from the 1852 article.
notes: The order route is actionable, but whole-object feasibility and cost require an NRO estimate and condition review.
openQuestions.question: Can complete, foliation-stable color facsimiles of NNAS S2/1/1-2 and NCR 17b/9 be obtained and mapped to CME00122?

next_steps: Inspect NCR 17b/9 and the 1389 guild return directly, including numeral forms and later additions.
Compare a modern critical edition of the guild records with Mackerell's manuscript and the 1852 print.
Do not silently normalize source readings without preserving each layer and its evidence.
openQuestions.question: What documentary or calendrical history explains the 1324/1385 foundation conflict and the edition's 1451 versus current-catalog 1452 date for Yelverton's Mediation?

next_steps: Inspect original issue wrappers and society proceedings rather than inferring issue dates from later catalog metadata.
Use 1852 for this inspected volume entity until distinct issue-level evidence is found.
Consider reporting the conflict to U-M and ADS.
openQuestions.question: Do original issue wrappers or society records establish an earlier installment date that explains U-M's 1851 and ADS's 1849 values despite the bound-volume title page dated 1852?

next_steps: Request a path-specific clarification from the repository maintainer.
Until clarified, preserve copyright status as unknown for the modern XML layer and do not broaden the software wording.
openQuestions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded root Unlicense intentionally apply to XML markup and data?

relations.subjectLabel: ADS Library record for Norfolk Archaeology 03 and the Mackerell article

reviewNotes: The primary subject is the repository artifact CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00122.xml. Mackerell's manuscript, the medieval guild book, the 1852 article, Internet Archive facsimile, U-M encoding, ADS catalog description, and modern thesis are separate entities.

CME00122 encodes the article heading through the end of Yelverton's Mediation at the top of printed p. 334. The immediate article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374; that continuation is omitted.

The complete source-volume mapping is printed p. 315 = Internet Archive PDF p. 773 = BookReader n775 and the encoded top portion of printed p. 334 = PDF p. 811 = n813. The full article continues through printed p. 374 = n893.

Mackerell's 'Book I, marked A' is strongly but not conclusively crosswalked to current NCR 17b/9. The exact f. 189 locus is independently supported only for Yelverton's Mediation; the first ordinances and patent retain unresolved exact loci.
The first represented text says the fraternity began in 1324, while the current NRO historical account says 1385. The normalized historical date is not inferred from the electronic text alone.

The 1852 edition prints 1451 for Yelverton's Mediation, while the current NRO catalog and Durkee date it 27 March 1452. The modern catalog date controls the normalized metadata while the edition reading remains recorded.
The inspected bound volume title page and Internet Archive metadata say 1852. U-M's publication field says 1851 and ADS labels the issue 1849; those provider conflicts remain explicit.

The NRO catalog records expose no digital objects. Exact-shelfmark searches found no public manuscript facsimile; the dated negative does not establish that NRO lacks an internal or request-deliverable surrogate.

The repository root LICENSE is the software-worded Unlicense. It contains no XML- or data-specific scope statement, so applicability to CME00122 markup/data is recorded as unknown rather than broadened into a confirmed public-domain dedication.

sourceLinks.format: Normally JPEG digital images; whole-object feasibility and specification by estimate
notes: The online service price is GBP 19.98 for up to six consecutive or sample images from one document; NRO gives an estimate for the rest and may charge GBP 60 per hour when more cost-effective.

For NCR 17b/9, specify a complete color object capture: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if offered. Ask NRO to preserve a stable image sequence and any foliation or pagination in the delivery.
Map the CME-related portions separately from that whole-object request: ask NRO to confirm the loci of the first charter and 1417 patent and to map Yelverton's Mediation at f. 189.

lineage.summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME00122, encoded from printed p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334 of Benjamin Mackerell's 'Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich' in Norfolk Archaeology 3 (1852), pp. 315-374. The article resumes lower on p. 334 after the encoded endpoint. Its Mackerell-derived passages excerpted his 1737 manuscript History of Norwich, now described by Norfolk Record Office as NNAS S2/1/1-2; the anonymous society title and framing headnote are editorial additions. Mackerell states that he copied the first charter from the company's Book I marked A and a later patent from a company book; the present NRO record NCR 17b/9 contains the matching charter/ordinance and agreement material. Modern scholarship identifies Yelverton's Mediation specifically at NCR 17b/9, f. 189. A complete 1,090-page Internet Archive PDF and all canonical full-volume OCR, metadata, page-map, and scandata derivatives are cached with fixity and exact selection mapping; the complete Durkee thesis supporting the f. 189 identification is cached separately. No legitimate public facsimile of either physical NRO manuscript was found.

catalogingSubject.label: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is the opening selection of Benjamin Mackerell's 1737 antiquarian account as anonymously introduced and printed by the Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society in 1852. CME00122 represents printed p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334, ending immediately after Yelverton's Mediation: a society source note, Mackerell's introduction, a Middle English charter-and-ordinance text, an English rendering of Henry V's 1417 letters patent, and the Middle English agreement known as Yelverton's Mediation. The immediate article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374, so it is not fully encoded. This record keeps the historical documents, Mackerell's two-volume manuscript History of Norwich, the 1852 article, the journal-volume scan, the U-M encoding, and the checked-in XML distinct.

contentParts.label: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society source headnote

DIV1/HEAD followed by two HEADNOTE/P elements

notes: This is nineteenth-century paratext, not Mackerell's prose or a medieval guild document.
contentParts.summary: The source line naming Mackerell's 1737 manuscript and the society's explanation that local interest justified publication.

contentParts.label: Mackerell's introduction, Of St. George's Company

locators.value: p. 315; Internet Archive PDF p. 773; BookReader n775
first DIV2 TYPE='editor's preface'; one HEAD and one P

contentParts.summary: Mackerell announces a full institutional history and says he copied the first charter from a company folio manuscript, Book I marked A.

statement of establishment by royal charter and aims, with ordinances; exact folio range unresolved

notes: The text's 1324 foundation statement conflicts with the current NRO account's 1385 date; no silent correction is made.
contentParts.summary: A Middle English charter-and-ordinance text describing the fraternity, royal confirmation, annual worship and feast, liveries and procession, offices and elections, discipline, alms, sickness, burial, and admission payments, including bracketed later additions.

contentParts.label: Henry V letters patent for the Guild of St George

locators.value: pp. 325-327; Internet Archive PDF pp. 793-797; BookReader n795-n799
royal-charter establishment statement; exact patent locus and source-book identity unresolved
second DIV2 direct P elements 45-52, two CLOSER elements, and TAILNOTE/P

notes: Mackerell says he found and transcribed this after his first transcription from a book belonging to the company, but the inspected sources do not name the translator or prove the exact modern locus.
contentParts.summary: Mackerell's transition followed by an English rendering of Henry V's letters patent confirming and incorporating the guild, authorizing officers, ordinances, a common seal, litigation, and property up to ten pounds yearly, dated 9 May 1417, with Wyndham signature and a Great Seal note.

contentParts.label: Yelverton's Mediation

locators.value: printed p. 328 through the top of p. 334; Internet Archive PDF pp. 799-811; BookReader n801-n813
f. 189 according to Durkee 2017, p. 179 n. 44; current NRO catalog describes a copy agreement dated 27 March 1452
third DIV2 TYPE='document'; one HEAD and 15 P elements

notes: The NRO record identifies the surviving item as a copy agreement, not the original indenture.
contentParts.summary: The agreement joining Norwich's mayoral and common-council offices to governance and membership of the Guild of St George, regulating worship, office, property, discipline, and annual reading of the articles.

dateStatements.display: 1737, as dated by the 1852 article heading and U-M item
notes: The current NRO record dates the two-volume manuscript only as undated, pre-1738; 1737 is the date assigned by the printed source and U-M.

assertion.notes: This is a documented conflict between the represented historical text and the archive's current account, not a date interval.

dateStatements.display: 1324 in the edited charter-and-ordinance text; 1385 in the current Norfolk Record Office historical account
27 March 1452 in the current NRO catalog; the 1852 edition prints 1451
notes: The current NRO item record and Durkee's modern citation control the normalized date; the edition's closing 1451 is preserved as a source conflict.
The inspected volume title page says 1852. U-M's publication field says 1851 and ADS assigns the issue 1849; those provider conflicts are retained rather than silently harmonized.

extent.description: The 71 modeled prose units are the XML's 71 P elements: two society headnote paragraphs, one Mackerell introduction paragraph, 44 first-charter/ordinance paragraphs, eight patent/transition paragraphs plus one tailnote paragraph, and 15 Yelverton paragraphs. The file also contains one DIV1, three DIV2, four HEAD, 21 PB, two CLOSER, one DATELINE, two DATE, one SIGNED, one TAILNOTE, four italic HI, and 65 SUP elements; it has no verse, NOTE, GAP, or UNCLEAR elements. Counts describe the encoding, not original medieval document structure.

formStatements.description: Antiquarian framing and documentary prose; no verse structures occur in the encoding.

genres.label: Edited selection
notes: CME00122 stops after Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334; the immediate article resumes lower on that page and continues through p. 374.

scope.description: Nineteenth-century society framing, Mackerell's early modern prose, and the English patent rendering

languageStatements.label: Middle English
notes: The XML applies TEXT/@LANG='enm' to the entire encoded selection, but its society and Mackerell framing and the patent rendering are later English; the manifest scopes language by content layer.
Mackerell's account, the three historical documentary layers, their surviving archival copies, the 1852 article, U-M encoding, and repository XML are modeled separately.
The exact checked-in XML is git blob d148227be1810385e2a1b37ea16ca254171cb4e1 at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
All counts and source-page boundaries were independently recomputed on 2026-07-13.

next_steps: Request an NRO item-level or folio-level crosswalk for the St George account within both Mackerell volumes.
Ask whether Book I/A is NCR 17b/9 and whether the patent and first ordinances share that source with Yelverton's Mediation.
Retain Yelverton's f. 189 link separately because it has modern scholarly support; do not extend that folio to the other documents.
openQuestions.question: Where exactly in NRO NNAS S2/1/1-2 does Mackerell's St George account occur, and can NRO confirm that his 'Book I, marked A' is current NCR 17b/9 and identify the exact loci for all three represented documents?

next_steps: Inspect NCR 17b/9 directly to determine the manuscript reading and documentary layer behind Mackerell's 1324 text.
Consult the 1389 guild return and modern editions before deciding whether 1324 is a scribal, transcriptional, typographical, or interpretive error.
Keep the represented text's claim distinct from the modern historical date.
openQuestions.question: Why does the first document printed by Mackerell date the fraternity's beginning to 1324 when the current NRO account and modern scholarship date it to 1385?

next_steps: Use NRO's online document-order form or contact norfrec@norfolk.gov.uk. For both Mackerell volumes and NCR 17b/9, request a complete color object capture that includes front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if NRO offers edge photography; ask for stable image-to-folio mapping.
Ask NRO first whether complete existing digital surrogates already exist and what delivery and publication terms apply.
If images are supplied, preserve the entire delivery and rights notice, then map the Mackerell account, NCR 17b/9 f. 189, and the other source loci without cropping the cached master.
notes: The official catalog records expose no digital objects, and exact-shelfmark web searches found no legitimate public facsimile; this is dated negative evidence, not proof that NRO has no internal surrogate.
openQuestions.question: Can complete, foliation-stable color facsimiles of both NNAS S2/1/1-2 and NCR 17b/9 be obtained and mapped to this work?

next_steps: Check original issue wrappers, society proceedings, and serial publication history for installment or issue dates.
Use 1852 for the inspected bound-volume citation unless issue-level evidence establishes a distinct earlier publication event.
Report the discrepancy to provider catalogs if appropriate.
openQuestions.question: Why do U-M and ADS assign 1851 and 1849 respectively when the inspected source volume's title page and Internet Archive metadata say 1852?

scope.description: Mackerell's antiquarian account and its selected transcriptions; the society headnote is excluded

assertion.notes: The fact of transcription is explicit; diplomatic fidelity and exact modern manuscript loci are not.

scope.description: Documents Mackerell says he copied or transcribed from books belonging to St George's Company

notes: No personal editor or article byline appears in the inspected volume; organizational editorial responsibility should not be converted into medieval authorship.

scope.description: Anonymous society selection, framing, and publication of the Mackerell account

subjects.label: Guild of St George in Norwich
Admission, profession, visitation, discipline, and community governance

summaries.value: An edited opening selection from Benjamin Mackerell's antiquarian history of Norwich's Guild of St George. After a society source note and Mackerell's introduction, it prints guild ordinances governing worship, livery, feasting, office, discipline, poor relief, and burial; an English rendering of Henry V's 1417 incorporation and mortmain patent; and the 1452 agreement that joined the guild's leadership and membership to Norwich civic office.

CME00122 begins with the article heading on printed p. 315 and ends after Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334. The source article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374 with Mackerell's discussion, extracted ordinances, office lists, pageantry, property, and dissolution; that remainder is not encoded.

Three date layers conflict in the sources. The first printed document says the fraternity began in 1324, while the current NRO historical account says 1385. The 1852 edition closes Yelverton's Mediation with 1451, while the current NRO catalog and modern scholarship date it 27 March 1452. The inspected volume title page says 1852, while U-M's publication field says 1851 and ADS labels the issue 1849.
